questions.question: Which CLI tool does Claude Code use under the hood to create GitHub pull requests?
questions.explanation: Claude Code uses the GitHub CLI (gh) to interact with GitHub. The command gh pr create handles pushing and opening pull requests.

questions.question: When reviewing a PR, which command does Claude use to see the code changes?
questions.explanation: gh pr diff <number> fetches the full diff of a pull request, which Claude then analyzes for issues.

questions.question: What is the FIRST thing you should do when reporting a bug to Claude Code?
options: Ask Claude to fix it immediately
Provide a clear description with expected vs actual behavior
Run git bisect to find the breaking commit
questions.explanation: Starting with a clear description of expected vs actual behavior, reproduction steps, and error messages gives Claude the context needed to investigate efficiently.

questions.question: Before starting a multi-file refactor, what should you ask Claude to do first?
options: Create a new branch and commit
List all files that would be affected by the change
Update the CLAUDE.md with refactoring instructions
questions.explanation: Planning before executing is critical for refactors. Ask Claude to identify all affected files so you can review the scope before any changes are made.

questions.question: After a codebase-wide rename, which verification steps should you run?
options: Only git diff to see the changes
questions.explanation: After a refactor, run the full verification pipeline: build (catches broken imports), lint (catches unused variables), and tests (catches behavioral regressions).

questions.question: What does Claude examine when you paste a stack trace?
options: Only the last line of the trace
The files at the top of the stack, tracing from error origin through propagation
questions.explanation: Claude reads the files referenced in the stack trace starting from the top (error origin), traces the propagation path, and identifies where the root cause is versus where the error surfaces.
